input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `External Items/External Item`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `External Items/External Item` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.5.11.3
            TITLE: external items should not point at the driver

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.11.3: External Items should not point at the driver
F.5.11.3 External Items should not point at the driver

RULE: f.5.11.2
            TITLE: external items must meet one of the two conditions:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.11.2: External Items must meet one of the two conditions:
F.5.11.2 External Items must meet one of the two conditions: 
a. Be attached at a Hoop to Brace node or a fully Triangulated structural node without the 
ability to create a moment at the node  
b. When not attached at a node as described above, then: 
• Additional Structural bracing meeting F.3.2.1.o must be added to prevent bending 
loads  
• Additional calculations must be performed to show the member will not fail in 
bending or shear, even if unbraced

RULE: f.11.2.4
            TITLE: non crushable items mounted behind the rear impact structure must not be able to come

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.4: Non Crushable Items mounted behind the Rear Impact structure must not be able to come
F.11.2.4 Non Crushable Items mounted behind the Rear Impact structure must not be able to come 
through the Rear Impact structure.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 54 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
T - TECHNICAL ASPECTS

RULE: s.3.8.2
            TITLE: if a team wishes to use any parts, processes or materials not included in the tables, an add

            CONTENT:
            rule S.3.8.2: If a team wishes to use any Parts, Processes or Materials not included in the tables, an Add
S.3.8.2 If a team wishes to use any Parts, Processes or Materials not included in the tables, an Add 
Item Request must be submitted.  See S.3.10
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 121 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.1.6.2
            TITLE: insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the driver’s seat or firewall.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.2: Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
T.1.6.2 Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 57 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: s.3.10.1
            TITLE: an add item request must be submitted on the fsae online website to add items to the cost

            CONTENT:
            rule S.3.10.1: An Add Item Request must be submitted on the FSAE Online Website to add items to the Cost
S.3.10.1 An Add Item Request must be submitted on the FSAE Online Website to add items to the Cost 
Tables for individual team requirements.

RULE: ev.4.3.4
            TITLE: the accumulator container(s) may contain holes or openings

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.3.4: The Accumulator Container(s) may contain Holes or Openings
EV.4.3.4 The Accumulator Container(s) may contain Holes or Openings 
a. Only the wiring harness, ventilation, cooling and fasteners may pass through holes in the 
Accumulator Container(s) 
b. Holes and Openings in the Accumulator Container must meet F.10.4   
c. External holes must meet EV.6.1

RULE: in.4.1
            TITLE: inspection items

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.4.1: Inspection Items
IN.4.1 Inspection Items 
Bring these to Electrical Technical Inspection: 
• Charger(s) for the Accumulator(s)  EV.8.1 
• Accumulator Container Hand Cart  EV.4.10 
• Spare Accumulator(s) (if applicable)  EV.5.1.4  
• Electrical Systems Form (ESF) and Component Data Sheets  EV.2  
• Copies of any submitted Rules Questions with the received answer  GR.7 
The following basic tools in good condition: 
• Insulated cable shears 
• Insulated screw drivers 
• Multimeter with protected probe tips 
• Insulated tools, if screwed connections are used in the Tractive System 
• Face Shield 
• HV insulating gloves which are 12 months or less from their test date  
• Two HV insulating blankets of minimum 0.83 m² each 
• Safety glasses with side shields for all team members that might work on the Tractive 
System or Accumulator

RULE: t.1.6.3
            TITLE: the design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.3: The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
T.1.6.3 The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples 
include but are not limited to:  exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and 
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor): 
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following: 
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel 
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm 
between the heat source and the panel  
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the 
panel 
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following: 
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm 
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation

RULE: in.2.6.1
            TITLE: officials may mark, seal, or designate items or areas which have been inspected to document

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.2.6.1: Officials may mark, seal, or designate items or areas which have been inspected to document
IN.2.6.1 Officials may mark, seal, or designate items or areas which have been inspected to document 
the inspection and reduce the chance of tampering

RULE: f.4.1.1
            TITLE: documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.1: Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)
F.4.1.1 Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation) 
and the material properties.

RULE: in.2.4.2
            TITLE: technical inspectors may examine any other items at their discretion

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.2.4.2: Technical Inspectors may examine any other items at their discretion
IN.2.4.2 Technical Inspectors may examine any other items at their discretion
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 110 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: f.5.11.1
            TITLE: any item which meets all three of:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.11.1: Any item which meets all three of:
F.5.11.1 Any item which meets all three of: 
• Located outside the Component Envelope  F.1.15 
• Located above 350 mm from the ground  
• Could load the Main Hoop, Main Hoop Brace, or Shoulder Harness Mounting during a 
rollover

RULE: t.1.8.1
            TITLE: requirement

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.1: Requirement
T.1.8.1 Requirement 
A Firewall(s) must separate the driver compartment and any portion of the Driver Harness 
from:  
a. All components of the Fuel System, the engine oil, the liquid cooling systems, any lithium 
batteries 
b. (EV only) All Tractive System components other than Outboard Wheel Motors EV.4.1.3 
where mounted at the wheels or on the front control arms

RULE: ic.7.2.3
            TITLE: any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.7.2.3: Any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in
IC.7.2.3 Any exhaust components (headers, mufflers, etc.) that protrude from the side of the body in 
front of the Main Hoop must be shielded to prevent contact by persons approaching the 
vehicle or a driver exiting the vehicle

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
F.11.2.2 Rear Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from rear impact:  
• When the rear impact structure is 100 mm or less from an Accumulator Container, 
the structure must be Equivalent to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
• When the rear impact structure is more than 100 mm from an Accumulator 
Container, the structure must meet  F.5.16 Component Protection 
• The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. The Rear Impact Protection for the Accumulator Container must: 
• Go to the Upper Side Impact Height at minimum
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 53 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the top of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the bottom of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Lower Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: t.1.4.3
            TITLE: all vehicle controls (steering, gear change, cockpit main switch / cockpit shutdown button)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.3: All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button)
T.1.4.3 All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button) 
must be operated from inside the cockpit without any part of the driver, including hands, arms 
or elbows, being outside of: 
a. The Side Impact Structure defined in F.6.4 / F.7.5 
b. Two longitudinal vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the 
uppermost member of the Side Impact Structure

RULE: f.11.2.3
            TITLE: all non crushable items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.3: All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the
F.11.2.3 All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the 
Accumulator Container. 
Non Crushable Items include, but are not limited to motors, differentials, and the side or rear 
impact structure itself.  Accumulator mounts do not require clearance

RULE: f.11.1.3
            TITLE: tractive system (ev.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in f.11.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.1.3: Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2
F.11.1.3 Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2 
above must be contained inside one or both of: 
• The Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13 
• Structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: f.11.2.1
            TITLE: side impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.1: Side Impact Protection
F.11.2.1 Side Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from side impact by structure Equivalent 
to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. Accumulator Container side impact protection must go to a minimum height that is the 
lower of the two:  
• The height of the Upper Side Impact Structure 
• The top of the Accumulator Container at that point 
c. Tractive System components other than Accumulator Containers in a position below 350 
mm from the ground must be protected from side impact by structure that meets F.5.16 
Component Protection
